articles.seedTopic: How creators can find brand deals without wasting time on low-fit opportunities
articles.audience: creators trying to build a steadier pipeline of realistic brand opportunities
articles.angle: Teach a repeatable discovery workflow that starts with niche fit, platform fit, and workload filtering before creators start pitching or applying.

articles.seedTopic: How UGC creators can find brand deals that fit their content style
articles.audience: UGC creators who want more relevant product briefs and fewer random offers
articles.angle: Focus on how UGC creators should filter opportunities by deliverables, posting requirements, usage rights, and sample-versus-cash compensation.

articles.seedTopic: How TikTok creators should qualify brand deals before accepting a brief
articles.audience: TikTok creators comparing short-form sponsorships, UGC asks, and product-led campaigns
articles.angle: Show how TikTok creators can score offers by hook potential, editing load, usage rights, and whether the product naturally fits fast-moving short-form content.

articles.seedTopic: What Instagram creators should check before saying yes to a paid collab
articles.audience: Instagram creators reviewing Reels, Stories, feed-post, and hybrid collaboration briefs
articles.angle: Break down how Instagram creators should compare visual fit, audience trust, exclusivity, and content reuse before they accept a paid collaboration.

articles.seedTopic: How YouTube creators should score sponsorship offers before they commit
articles.audience: YouTube creators weighing dedicated videos, sponsor integrations, reviews, and Shorts briefs
articles.angle: Teach a decision framework around product depth, production time, talking points, approval friction, and ad-usage expectations for video creators.

articles.seedTopic: How beauty creators should filter brand deals for fit, workload, and trust
articles.audience: beauty creators sorting through makeup, skincare, haircare, and review-driven collaborations
articles.angle: Show how beauty creators can shortlist better opportunities by product fit, audience expectations, claims sensitivity, and whether the deal is gifted, paid, or ad-usage heavy.

articles.seedTopic: How creators should evaluate skincare brand deals without taking on risky claims
articles.audience: skincare creators reviewing product campaigns that involve routines, claims, reviews, or before-and-after content
articles.angle: Focus on claim sensitivity, testing period, deliverable scope, audience trust, and when a skincare collaboration creates more downside than upside.

articles.seedTopic: How Australia-based creators can find paid collabs that are actually a fit
articles.audience: creators in Australia trying to filter opportunities by shipping region, audience location, and campaign logistics
articles.angle: Explain how Australian creators should qualify regional campaigns, global briefs, shipping limitations, and local audience fit before applying.

articles.seedTopic: Are free product collabs worth it for creators? A practical decision checklist
articles.audience: creators weighing gifted campaigns, sample offers, and product-seeding requests
articles.angle: Help creators compare product value, production workload, usage rights, and when gifted-only campaigns should become paid asks instead.

articles.seedTopic: How micro influencers can get brand deals without looking bigger than they are
articles.audience: small and mid-size creators trying to win more relevant sponsorships through niche fit
articles.angle: Show how micro influencers should use audience trust, category fit, and realistic deliverables to compete for deals without pretending to be larger creators.
